decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest priority lever to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the peak temperature.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 20% (within the 2.0x limit) improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly addressing the high severity temperature violation.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications (though not constrained here)
